name: Impact Credit Union, INC.
description_short: Federally insured state-chartered credit union headquartered in Clyde, OH with $200M in assets, 14,217 members, and 5 branch locations offering standard banking and financial services.
description_long: Impact Credit Union, Inc. is a federally insured state-chartered credit union that has served its membership for 32 years since opening in 1994. Headquartered in Clyde, Ohio, the institution has grown to manage approximately $200.46 million in total assets and serves about 14,217 members across 5 branch locations throughout northwest Ohio. The organization employs 54 full and part-time employees and is led by CEO Scott Hicks and Chairperson Troy Hashbarger. Impact Credit Union offers traditional banking and financial services typical of credit union membership, including checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, and various loan products. Their loan portfolio reflects a balanced approach, with $122.6 million in total loans distributed across real estate loans ($55.2M), unsecured loans ($7M), and other loan categories ($60.5M). The institution provides first-time homebuyer programs and low-cost wire transfers as specialized offerings. As a federally insured state-chartered credit union with FHLB membership status, Impact Credit Union operates under regulatory oversight by the NCUA and maintains NCUSIF deposit insurance protection for members. The credit union is classified as a natural person membership institution, meaning it serves individuals rather than exclusively corporate or occupational groups. With assets of $200.5 million and member deposits totaling $180.9 million, the organization maintains a solid equity position of $17.4 million and reported net income of $685,130 in the most recent audit period. Impact Credit Union represents a standard regional credit union option for consumers in northwest Ohio seeking traditional banking services. The institution maintains consistent operations with a last audit date of December 1, 2024, and demonstrates operational stability through positive net income. However, as a mid-sized regional credit union, it may have more limited product innovation or technology features compared to larger national institutions or fintech competitors.
services: Money Market or other deposit accounts (All Other Shares & Deposits)
pros: Federally insured state-chartered institution with NCUSIF deposit protection for member safety
32-year operational history demonstrating institutional stability and long-term member relationships
5 branch locations across northwest Ohio providing convenient access for regional members
Offers first-time homebuyer programs, a valuable service for entry-level home purchasers
Low-cost wire transfer services, reducing expenses for members conducting money transfers
FHLB membership providing access to additional funding and liquidity resources
Positive net income of $685,130 with healthy equity position of $17.4 million
cons: Regional presence limited to northwest Ohio, not suitable for consumers needing national branch access
Likely limited digital banking and mobile app capabilities compared to larger national credit unions
No mention of credit builder loans, secured credit cards, or credit-building products on website
No investment products listed despite having investment income, suggesting limited wealth management services
Mid-sized institution may have fewer specialized loan products or competitive rates compared to larger alternatives
best_for: Northwest Ohio residents seeking traditional checking and savings accounts with local branch access
First-time homebuyers in the Clyde, Ohio area looking for dedicated home purchase financing programs
Credit union members prioritizing federal deposit insurance and stable, long-established institutions
Business owners and individuals needing affordable wire transfer services and ATM/debit card access
diagnosis: Impact Credit Union is best for northwest Ohio residents seeking traditional, federally-insured banking services with local branch access and stable, long-term operations. The primary caveat is its limited regional footprint and lack of evidence for digital innovation or specialized credit-building products, making it less suitable for consumers needing advanced technology or those relocating outside Ohio.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Federally insured state-chartered credit union in Clyde, OH with $200M in assets, 14,217 members, and 5 branches offering banking and financial services.
